executive_summary: Business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 crisis. Net margin £1,525,682 (8.1% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark). Hedging cost £4,223,875 vs going naked (85% mandate price). 489 bill shock events (118 in crisis years); 12/12 retention offers accepted.

insights.headline: Hedging cost £4,223,875 vs going naked (85% mandate price)
insights.narrative: Whole-run commodity basis: actual net £1,436,697 vs naked £5,660,572. The 85% minimum hedge mandate cost £4,223,875 over the 10-year window. Worst year: 2022 (hedging cost £1,022,784 vs naked). Hedging reduces variance (survival objective) at the cost of expected P&L.

insights.headline: 489 bill shock events (118 in crisis years); 12/12 retention offers accepted
insights.narrative: Bill shocks across 2016-2025: 489 events, 11 extreme (>100%). 2021-22 accounted for 118 shocks as wholesale prices spiked. Retention: 12/12 offers accepted (100%); 6 customers churned without an offer (margin guard blocked). Enterprise value: £8,160,654 across 15 accounts.

insights.headline: Risk committee: 38 interventions; SURVIVED full 2016-2025 window
insights.narrative: The risk committee (Context Handshake) intervened 38 times over 10 years, with 9 in the 2021-22 crisis. Business survived: No administration event -- Ofgem solvency floor maintained.. The 85% minimum hedge mandate limited worst-case exposure.

insights.headline: Net margin £1,525,682 (8.1% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark)
insights.narrative: Gross margin £6,433,656, net £1,525,682 (8.1% of revenue). Industry benchmark: 2-5% -- this business sits above that range. I&C customers contribute 99% of net margin (£1,515,629); residential 1% (£10,053). WARNING: I&C accounts for 99% of margin -- high concentration risk. Cost to serve: £18,727; net after CTS: £6,414,930.

insights.headline: 1550 bills issued; service quality 0.903/1.0, clarity 0.812/1.0
insights.narrative: 1550 bills issued over 2016-2025 (avg 155/yr). Service quality: 0.903 (excellent). Bill clarity: 0.812 (good). Cost to serve: £18,727 portfolio total, £1,248/account/run.

coherence_narrative: The dominant theme is crisis survival. The business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 energy crisis, with the 85% hedge mandate cost £4,223,875 vs going naked, 118 bill shocks in the 2021-22 crisis, and 12/12 retention offers accepted. net margin £1,525,682 (8.1% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark), with I&C customers contributing 99% of net margin. The hedging mandate limited worst-case downside at the cost of expected P&L in calmer years; the I&C book was the primary profit engine throughout. Without I&C revenue the residential book would be loss-making -- concentration risk is the key strategic vulnerability.
recommended_actions: I&C concentration critical (99% of margin): target acquisition of 2 additional I&C customers to reduce dependency
Hedge mandate cost £4,223,875 vs going naked: review minimum hedge fraction for non-crisis years (current 85% floor)
118 crisis-year bill shocks: consider tariff smoothing mechanism to spread 2021-22 wholesale spike across 2020-2023 for residential customers
